resources.name: Goethe-Institut Deutsch üben
resources.why: The free practice hub of Germany's official cultural institute: several hundred exercises, videos, podcasts and games sorted by level from A1 to C2, from the same body that writes the exam you are working towards. The obvious first place to look when you want more of something this app has just taught you.

resources.name: Goethe-Institut, Prüfungen und Anmeldung
resources.why: The official exam catalogue: every level from A1 to C2, what each one tests, who may sit it, and where to register. Go here when you are ready to book the real B1, and to check the current format before exam day, since formats do change.

resources.name: DW Learn German
resources.why: Deutsche Welle's free A1 to B1 course, built around a filmed story about a young man moving to Germany, with a placement test, short video episodes and interactive exercises. Free, complete to exactly the level this course targets, and made by Germany's international broadcaster.

resources.name: BAMF, Integrationskurse
resources.why: The federal office's own page on integration courses, which combine language teaching with an orientation course on life and law in Germany, and end in an official test. If you live in Germany, read this to find out whether you are entitled to a subsidised place, and use the BAMF-NAvI finder to locate one nearby.

resources.name: Easy German
resources.why: Street interviews with real people, filmed at natural speed with both German and English subtitles, plus a Super Easy German series pitched at A1 and a podcast for listening on the move. The best available bridge between textbook German and the German people actually speak to each other.

resources.name: Schritte international Neu (Hueber)
resources.why: The A1 to B1 coursebook series used in integration courses and in classrooms worldwide, with built-in preparation for Start Deutsch and the Zertifikat B1. Worth buying the level you are on if you want paper exercises and a second explanation of the same grammar in a different voice.

resources.name: Conversation partner
resources.why: Nothing in this list replaces a person who will let you be slow and wrong. A tandem partner, a colleague, a neighbour, or a paid tutor once a week: pick someone who will correct you rather than politely switch to English, and the speaking module stops being the one you fear.

resources.name: Words tab (built-in daily flashcards)
resources.why: The spaced repetition already inside this app. Everything above is optional; this is not. Ten minutes of Words a day is what moves German from something you have studied to something you can produce under exam pressure.
